content:
From Spaghetti Program.cs to Feature-Screaming, Testable, Maintainable Endpoints

11 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Minimal APIs in .NET are powerful. One file, twenty lines, and you have a running HTTP server. But that power becomes a liability when your Program.cs grows to 500 lines, your routes are scattered across extension methods with no discoverability, and your business logic bleeds into lambda expressions that can't be unit tested.
Clean Architecture solves the “where does this go?” problem. Minimal APIs solve the “why is there so much ceremony?” problem. Combine them correctly, and you get the best of both: zero boilerplate at the HTTP layer, strict dependency boundaries at the domain layer, and code that screams what it does.
This article shows you three battle-tested approaches — from simple to enterprise-grade — with complete code for each.
The Problem: Minimal APIs Without Structure
// Program.cs — the "everything file" anti-pattern
var builder = WebApplication.CreateBuilder(args);
var app = builder.Build();
app.MapGet("/products", async (AppDbContext db) =>
await db.Products.ToListAsync());
app.MapGet("/products/{id}", async (int id, AppDbContext…
